# Figure description: скв_398.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/drill_holes_data/скв_398.jpg`  
**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type

This is a **borehole/well log (геологический разрез скважины / каротажная диаграмма)** — a composite well log typical of Soviet-era geological survey practice. It displays multiple geophysical logging curves alongside a lithological column and supporting data tables, recorded continuously with depth. At the bottom of the sheet there is a separate section labeled **"КОНТРОЛЬНЫЕ КРИВЫЕ"** (control curves), which is a quality-control repeat section of the logging runs.

---

## Coverage and Subject

The figure header reads **"УЧ-К ТОСТЫ-ТОЛБЫ"** (участок Тосты-Толбы — the Tosty-Tolby prospect/site area), consistent with a location in northern Kazakhstan within the regional survey area of map sheets M-42/N-42. The document header also references **"КПП — Казахская ГПП"** (Kazakhskaya Geophysical Prospecting Party), with a surveyor name that appears to be **"Смолянова Г.П."** or similar [partially illegible].

The log covers a **substantial depth interval** — the depth scale runs continuously from near-surface down to what appears to be approximately **800–1000+ metres** (exact terminal depth [illegible] due to image quality). The borehole number is not clearly legible in the header but may be referenced in the column headers.

Curve labels visible in the upper portion of the log include:
- **КС** (КС — кажущееся сопротивление, apparent resistivity)
- **Каротаж** (general logging header)
- References to **КВН** or similar notation [partially illegible]
- **Кавернометрия №1** (caliper log №1) — labeled mid-log
- **Каротаж №2** (Logging run №2) — labeled mid-log

---

## Key Content

The composite log contains the following identifiable tracks/columns (left to right):

**Left margin columns (tabular data):**
- Stratigraphic/formation assignment column (narrow, with hatched/ruled symbols indicating lithological units)
- Depth column in metres
- Core recovery or sample description columns
- Lithological symbol column showing a **continuous stratigraphic/lithological section** rendered in standard Soviet hatching conventions — alternating patterns suggest interbedded sequences of varying rock types (likely alternating sandstones, siltstones, mudstones/clays, and possibly volcanics or carbonates based on pattern types visible)

**Central geophysical curve tracks:**
Two main waveform curves are plotted side by side throughout the full depth range:
1. **Left curve** — appears to be a **spontaneous potential (ПС, самопроизвольная поляризация)** or a short-spacing resistivity curve, showing moderate amplitude fluctuations throughout
2. **Right curve** — appears to be a **standard resistivity (КС)** or induction log curve with higher amplitude deflections, showing pronounced spikes and anomalies at several depth intervals

**Notable features in the curves:**
- In the **upper third** of the log (shallower depths), both curves show relatively subdued, low-amplitude variation
- In the **middle section**, both curves become significantly more active with **high-amplitude resistivity spikes** — suggesting harder, more resistive formations (possibly crystalline basement, carbonates, or volcanic rocks)
- Several **sharp positive deflections** on the resistivity curve stand out as anomalous intervals — these could indicate tight/dense formations, mineralized zones, or lithological boundaries
- The **caliper curve** (кавернометрия) where labeled shows borehole diameter variations, with some washout zones visible
- Toward the **lower third**, curve amplitudes remain elevated but somewhat more uniform

**Right margin columns (tabular data):**
- Multiple narrow columns with numerical values — likely porosity, density, or geophysical parameter tables recorded at specific depth intervals
- Hatched pattern columns continuing the lithological log
- A column that appears to record **formation/horizon picks** or interval descriptions [largely illegible at this resolution]

**Bottom section — КОНТРОЛЬНЫЕ КРИВЫЕ:**
A repeat logging section showing two shortened curve traces for quality control verification, confirming the reproducibility of the main log measurements.

## Research Value

This composite borehole log for the Tosty-Tolby prospect provides a continuous record of subsurface lithology and petrophysical properties through a deep section, enabling correlation of geophysical anomalies (particularly high-resistivity intervals in the middle and lower sections) with specific lithological units that may represent mineralized or structurally significant horizons.
